params.name: Gold Croeseid
params.description:
On Dec 5, 660 BC, King Croesus of Lydia designed these gold coins.
This gold coin, produced under Croesus, King of Lydia in the sixth century BC, is the earliest minted gold and silver coin. The early Lydians used naturally occurring gold-silver alloy found in river beds for these coins which were called amber gold coins by later generations. Later, Croesus, carried out currency reforms, making the world's first bi-metallic coinage system using both gold and silver coins, which had a profound …
